pages.full_text:
Moor End Farm
Ashperton

On to the farm where we looked at the excavations for the big new hop barn structure
William complained about the engineer doing detailed design of the barn.
	Freya met us & we walked up to the yurt. I had given Jenny + Nina + Briony presents from Japan. Gilda + Sally + their twin boys were visiting for the w[ee]kend. The yurt is being opened. Freya is going to manage Charles' + Jenny's wedding – 'if they get to marry at Belmont Abbey + reception at the Farm. Belmont so uncertain....
	We moved off for lunch down a lane near Belmont Abbey. All local people very helpful. Lots of may out + elderflo[wers]
pages.notes: Page contains a glued-in cheese label: 'Lightwood Award Winning English Cheese – Bromyard Brie – A creamy soft cows' milk cheese – Ingredients: Cows' milk, double cream, rennet, salt – Suitable for vegetarians – Keep refrigerated – UK MH005 EC – Heath Grange Farm, Grange Lane, Lower Broadheath, Worcester WR2 6RW'. Handwritten text is in purple/violet ink. The word 'structure' appears written in a slightly different hand or as an addition. Top of page text ('Moor End Farm / Ashperton') is partially cut off. Text around the label continues the narrative about visiting the farm and hop barn excavations. The word 'elderflo[wers]' is cut off at the right edge. No handwritten page number visible on this side.

pages.full_text:
After lunch we looked around the Abbey. Charles put on a clean, very creased shirt & covered it up with a jacket. Charles + Jenny had an interview with Father Nick who says they may get married at Belmont Abbey. Hooray! Next June or July.
	After tea, when Sonja sounded out the hotel there, we left for Moor End Farm Campsite. The farmer lent us a map + we walked thru fields of sheep + broad beans to Ashperton Castle Moat + looked round the outside of the church. The nice chair of PCC, who came to this part of Herefordshire from the other side to be a milk maid a long time ago told us [the] Eng. Heritage will only let them drain + repair [1292] Moat if they don't use mechanical digger, so it is deteriorating.
	Lovely field. A Canal (built 1790–1849)
2 phase build
pages.notes: Handwritten page number 86 at top right. Text in purple/violet ink with some words struck through (e.g. 'the' before 'Eng. Heritage' appears struck through). The annotation '1292' appears written above 'Moat' — possibly the date the moat was built. The 'Lovely field. A Canal (built 1790–1849) / 2 phase build' text at the bottom appears to relate to a canal nearby. A decorative yellow/gold wheat/flower motif (sticker or stamp) appears twice on this spread. 'Father Nick' is the priest at Belmont Abbey interviewed by Charles and Jenny about their wedding. The year cannot be precisely determined from this page alone; based on context (mention of potential upcoming wedding 'next June or July'), likely mid-2000s to early 2010s.
